metadata.name: NovaTrek Microservice Pattern
metadata.description: Every NovaTrek microservice must own exactly one database, communicate via HTTPS or Kafka, and declare domain ownership.

rules.description: Each service connects to exactly one database, and each database is owned by exactly one service.
rules.rationale: Shared databases create tight coupling between services and violate bounded context boundaries. See ADR-010, ADR-011.

rules.description: Services must communicate via HTTPS (REST) or Kafka (events). No direct JDBC connections between services.
rules.rationale: Direct database access between services bypasses API contracts, making independent deployment impossible.

rules.description: Every service node must declare its domain and owning team in metadata.
rules.rationale: Domain classification drives the portal structure, team routing, and bounded context enforcement.

rules.description: Every service should participate in at least one relationship (caller or callee).
rules.rationale: An orphan service with no integrations may indicate incomplete modeling or a dead service.
